title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - High: [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L39) auto-pushes on every detected `git commit`. That is an external publication side effect without explicit approval, and it can trigger even after a failed commit because success is inferred from `stdout` text only at [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L27), not exit status or `stderr`. - High: [canon-build-cicd.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L119) and [canon-build-debug.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L103) fall back to `eval "$task"` when Codex is unavailable/not in a repo. These panes are described as natural-language REPLs, so this becomes arbitrary shell execution from plain-text input. - Medium: [canon-build-cicd.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-cicd.sh#L119) and [canon-build-debug.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L103) append `|| true`, then read `$?` afterward. In shell mode, failures are always reported as success, so the failure counters/backoff logic never engage. - Medium: [canon-build-debug.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L11) and [canon-build-debug.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-debug.sh#L61) track all monitored files with one `LAST_LOG_SIZE`. After the first file updates it, offsets for the remaining files are wrong, so new errors can be skipped or tailed from the wrong byte. I ignored the generated `experience.jsonl` / `.project-os` churn and focused on executable changes.
